title: Dialect Portability, NoSQL Tradeoffs, Distribution, and Production Defense · Query Repair Bay
summary: A team must move a browser prototype toward PostgreSQL, justify any non-relational component, and defend the complete data system. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a production-ready cross-dialect data system portfolio and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Separate standard relational intent from SQLite and PostgreSQL syntax, typing, NULL, date, and feature behavior.
Choose relational, document, key-value, graph, or mixed models from access, integrity, evolution, and consistency needs.
Explain replication, partitioning, distributed transactions, consistency choices, and failure boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Dialect Portability
steps.instruction:
debug case — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 debug artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 4; justify any non-relational component.

Inspect the Dialect Portability trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Dialect Portability.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate standard relational intent from sqlite and postgresql syntax, typing, null, date, and feature behavior. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Dialect Portability, keep Dialect Portability connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: For Dialect Portability, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Dialect Portability, name the row grain, Dialect Portability precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Dialect Portability, trace this concrete input and predict rows or changes before running it: Move a SQLite 3.49.1 debug artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 4; justify any non-relational component.
For Inspect the failure record · Debug Dialect Portability, challenge the documented misconception with result, constraint, or plan evidence: SQL behaves identically across all conforming databases.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Capstone Defense
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-ik2frh" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; dialect, storage-model, distribution, or production evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Capstone Defense.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Capstone Defense into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Capstone Defense, keep Capstone Defense connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-capstone-defense-ik2frh
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT typeof(owner_id) AS sqlite_runtime_type, COUNT(*) AS rows
FROM tickets GROUP BY typeof(owner_id) ORDER BY sqlite_runtime_type;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Capstone Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Capstone Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Move a SQLite 3.49.1 debug artifact toward PostgreSQL 18.4 while preserving relational intent. Record one dialect difference, consistency boundary, restore signal, and changed case 2; justify any non-relational component.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Capstone Defense.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Capstone Defense, keep Capstone Defense connected to executable relational evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend schema, queries, mutations, transactions, security, performance, portability, recovery, and ethical limits under changed evidence.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Capstone Defense, name the row grain, Capstone Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Capstone Defense, challenge the documented misconception with result, constraint, or plan evidence: Passing the original demo proves the database system is production ready.
